content.full_text:
92-276

GATEWAY TO LAKE BERRYESSA

City of Winters

MAYOR: Robert Chapman
VICE MAYOR: Keith Fridde
COUNCIL:
Frank Curry
Roger Mosher
Bill Planner
TREASURER: Margaret Dozier
CITY CLERK: Nanci Mills
CITY MANAGER: Perry Beck

FOUNDED IN 1875
318 First Street
Ph. 795-4910

Winters, California 95694

May 26, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 480
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Opinion Request - Winters Planning
     Commissioner Bruce Brewer

Attn: Blanca Breeze, Staff Attorney

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Enclosed is Mr. Brewer's Form 721, for your files. In response to your telephone inquiry, I have the following additional information to help with your advice:

1. Mr. Brewer has not, in fact, ever purchased a lot in Winters for construction of a single-family residence.

2. The local-builder or local owner-builder set aside policy of the City allows any local citizen, whether a contractor or not, to purchase lots.

Please let me know if you need additional information from me.

Very truly yours,

JOHN C. WALLACE
City Attorney
CITY OF WINTERS

JCW/j
enc.

CAPITOL OFFICE ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL BUILDING
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
PHONE: (916) 445-7222

DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESS
4667 MACARTHUR BOULEVARD,
SUITE 201
NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92660
PHONE (714) 756-0665

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES
HOUSING AND
COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT
EDUCATION
INSURANCE
TRANSPORTATION

GILBERT W. FERGUSON
ASSEMBLYMAN, SEVENTIETH DISTRICT

April 29, 1992

Mr. Ben Davidian, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #700
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Davidian:

In 1986, I became involved in a local city council race by
speaking out in favor of candidates and in opposition to the
incumbents.

An individual, the head of a local group, put out a hit piece
on one of the incumbents. The incumbent, after losing the
election, filed a lawsuit against the individual.

During the deposition, the individual said that he had shown
the piece to me before it was printed. The ex-councilmember then
amended his suit to include me as a defendant.

I immediately hired a lawyer to represent me.

During the early part of the trial I was deleted as a
defendant and shortly thereafter the Judge threw the case out and
all the charges were dropped.

Naturally, my lawyer must be paid. I gave him my personal
check to cover his retainer, about $2000. Subsequent to my
release from the trial, he sent me a bill for the amount he felt I
still owed him.

Because of the recession, it has been difficult for me to pay
him. He also, because of the recession needs the money. It
amounts to a little over $1000. He has said he will settle for
$426.00 cash if I can pay him immediately.

I would like to do so, however, it appears to me that I got
involved in this issue only because of my political position. I
responded to news inquiries and other public inquiries of me as
the local Assemblyman, by showing my opposition to the incumbents.

Printed on Recycled Paper

Page 2
April 29, 1992

So, I am reasoning that I should be able to pay my lawyer
from my campaign funds.

I would greatly appreciate your answer at your earliest
opportunity.

A. Can I pay him the $462.00 from my campaign funds and;

B. Can I be reimbursed, from my campaign funds for the
previous retainer which I paid for with personal funds.

Thanks for your attention to my questions.

Sincerely,

GIL FERGUSON
Assemblyman, 70th District

sections.question: A. Can I Pay him the $462.00 from my campaign funds and; B. Can I be reimbursed, from my campaign funds for the previous retainer which I paid for with personal funds?
sections.question_synthetic: An Assemblyman who incurred legal defense costs in a lawsuit stemming from his involvement in a local city council race seeks to know whether he can pay his lawyer from campaign funds and whether he can be reimbursed for a prior retainer paid with personal funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This appears to be a compilation of two different documents in one file - a brief cover letter from City of Winters and a longer incoming request from an Assemblyman. Both are incoming requests to FPPC, not FPPC responses. The second request (Ferguson letter) is the primary substantive document. Document structure is fragmented with no clear Q/C sections; confidence reduced due to formatting issues and document composition.

embedding.summary: This document contains two separate incoming requests to the FPPC: one from the City Attorney of Winters regarding Planning Commissioner Bruce Brewer's disclosure form, and one from Assemblyman Gilbert W. Ferguson seeking guidance on whether legal defense costs from a lawsuit can be paid or reimbursed using campaign funds.
